description:
Goal
Make compiler2 activation convergence behave like dynamic partitioning over semantic facts. Activation keys carry only the identity distinctions needed to do the job; `ActivationInputs` carries joined body evidence; cyclic activation components ascend through finite semantic domains; acyclic activations may replace stale broad evidence with sharper current evidence.

Signal
- The current dirty foundation is landed or split without mixed-ticket edits.
- Dynamic higher-order/protocol cycles are recognized from activation dataflow, not from `Recursive(function)` source-shape analysis.
- `AnalyzeActivation` publishes job effects from stored joined semantic evidence, so a narrow rerun cannot retract facts that are still part of the stored frontier.
- `SealSemanticClosure` checks the same settled facts it reports to the scheduler, without causing backend/native re-demand churn.
- Quicksort, Enum.reduce/range/list, and Enum.to_list/map converge under hard timeouts with explicit meaningful-event budgets.
- Static `Recursive(function)` is deleted from semantic convergence decisions or has a remaining named, tested purpose outside convergence.
- Docs and deletion checks prove old concepts are gone rather than renamed: recursive-only key widening, list-only convergence class, raw closure-capture default keys, transient retained-output patches, dishonest seal readiness, and unbounded telemetry workflows.

Strategy
1. `fz-rh2.18.9.5`: land or split the current activation-partition foundation from the dirty worktree.
2. `fz-rh2.18.9.7`: classify root-scoped activation dataflow cycles dynamically from semantic facts.
3. `fz-rh2.18.9.8`: make `AnalyzeActivation` project outputs from stored joined state, not transient observations.
4. `fz-rh2.18.9.10`: restore honest `SealSemanticClosure` settled reads without re-demand churn.
5. `fz-rh2.18.9.9`: prove Enum.to_list/map settles under a hard telemetry budget and that the wider compiler2 suite progresses.
6. `fz-rh2.18.9.6`: close the arc with docs, measured budgets, and no-go deletion checks before ReturnEvidence work begins.

```mermaid
graph LR
  A[fz-rh2.18.9.5 foundation / dirty split] --> B[fz-rh2.18.9.7 dynamic activation cycles]
  B --> C[fz-rh2.18.9.8 stored-state publication]
  C --> D[fz-rh2.18.9.10 honest seal settled reads]
  D --> E[fz-rh2.18.9.9 Enum map budget]
  E --> F[fz-rh2.18.9.6 docs and deletion contract]
```

Outro
No recursive-only convergence switch, no function-level proxy for activation cycles, no list-only `convergence_class`, no raw closure-capture identity unless policy proves it necessary, no transient retained-output workaround, no dishonest seal readiness, no quicksort/Enum special case, no broad top/bottom type used as readiness, and no unbounded telemetry command.
title: Compiler2 activation partitioning: key semantic work by necessary identity
